title: Milvus vector search times out on queries returning top-k > 10
description: Vector search queries with top_k greater than 10 consistently time out after 30 seconds under moderate load. Applied temporary workaround by increasing the client-side gRPC timeout to 60 seconds. Root cause is not fully diagnosed — suspected to be suboptimal IVF_FLAT index configuration with nlist=32. Workaround resolves symptoms but does not fix the underlying performance regression.

title: Ollama crashes with OOM when embedding batch size exceeds 8 items
description: The bge-m3 embedding service crashes with an out-of-memory error when batch size exceeds 8 items on the 32GB host. Workaround applied: reduced batch size to 4, which prevents crashes but cuts throughput by 50%. Root cause is suspected to be unbounded memory allocation during model warm-up when multiple requests run concurrently.

title: Postgres connection pool exhausted under concurrent ingestion load
description: Running the ingestion script with more than 8 parallel workers exhausts the SQLAlchemy connection pool and causes workers to deadlock waiting for a free connection. Pool max_overflow was incorrectly left at the default of 0. Increasing pool_size to 15 and max_overflow to 5 resolves the contention.

title: Embedding service returns HTTP 500 for queries containing special characters
description: Queries containing characters such as backslash, null byte, or Unicode surrogates cause the Ollama HTTP endpoint to return a 500 error without a meaningful message. The embedding client does not sanitize input before sending. Adding a pre-processing step that strips control characters and normalizes Unicode resolves the issue.

title: Reranker module produces NaN confidence scores for empty document chunks
description: When the retriever returns a document chunk with an empty string body — which can happen when Confluence pages have only image content — the reranker's dot product computation produces NaN. This propagates through the scoring pipeline and causes the answer agent to receive malformed context. A guard clause that filters out zero-length chunks before reranking resolves the issue.

title: Planner agent fails to correctly decompose multi-hop queries
description: Queries that require retrieving from both Jira and Confluence in a single pipeline run — such as 'What is the current fix for the Milvus timeout bug and where is the updated runbook?' — result in the Planner generating only a single retrieval sub-task. The prompt template for the Planner is missing explicit instructions to produce separate sub-tasks when multiple source types are implied by the query.

title: Milvus collection schema migration drops existing vector data silently
description: Attempting to alter a Milvus collection schema — specifically adding a new scalar field — requires dropping and recreating the collection. The migration script did not check for existing data before dropping, causing unrecoverable data loss in a staging environment. The script now checks collection row count and aborts if data is present, requiring an explicit backup flag to proceed.

title: Docker Compose Milvus health check fails intermittently on cold start
description: The Docker Compose health check for the Milvus standalone container uses a fixed 10-second initial delay, which is insufficient when the host is under I/O load during startup. The container is marked unhealthy and dependent services fail to start. Increasing start_period to 30 seconds and adding a retry loop in the health check script resolves the race condition.

title: Answer agent hallucinates dates when retrieved context is empty
description: When the retriever returns zero documents — for example on an out-of-domain query — the answer agent still produces a confident-sounding answer that fabricates specific dates and ticket numbers. The prompt template for the answering agent must be updated to include an explicit instruction to respond 'No relevant documents found' when the context block is empty rather than generating a best-guess response.

title: Retriever returns duplicate document chunks from the same source document
description: When a document is ingested multiple times — due to a retry after a partial failure — it creates duplicate embeddings in Milvus. The retriever then returns multiple identical chunks, inflating context and wasting token budget. A deduplication step using a content hash check before ingestion prevents double-insertion.

title: OpenRouter API call hangs indefinitely when rate limit is hit
description: When the OpenRouter API returns a 429 Too Many Requests response, the current HTTP client does not check the response status before waiting for a streaming body, causing the thread to hang until the OS-level TCP timeout fires (up to 5 minutes). Adding an explicit status check and raising an exception on 4xx responses, combined with retry logic, resolves the hang.

title: Vector similarity search returns incorrect neighbors after Milvus hot reload
description: After restarting the Milvus container without flushing the in-memory buffer, some recently ingested vectors are not persisted to disk and are absent from the index. The search then returns stale or incorrect neighbors for affected query vectors. Calling collection.flush() before stopping the container and loading the collection explicitly after restart resolves the consistency issue.

title: Scalar metadata filter ignored in hybrid Milvus search implementation
description: The hybrid search function passes the vector search expression parameter as a positional argument in the wrong order, causing scalar filters such as 'source_type == "jira"' to be silently ignored. All document types are then returned regardless of the specified filter. Fixing the argument order in the search() call aligns behavior with the Milvus Python SDK specification.

title: Confluence page content silently truncated at 512 tokens during ingestion
description: The ingestion script passes Confluence page body text through a tokenizer before chunking, but applies a hard cap of 512 tokens at the page level rather than the chunk level. Long pages are silently truncated before chunking, resulting in incomplete embeddings. The cap should be applied per-chunk after the sliding-window splitter runs, not before.

title: Jira ticket priority field not persisted in Milvus scalar column
description: The Milvus collection schema defines a VARCHAR field for 'priority' but the ingestion script maps the JSON field 'priority' to the wrong key name 'ticket_priority', causing the field to always ingest as null. Queries that filter by priority return no results. Fixing the key name in the field mapping dictionary resolves the issue.

title: Reranker scores exceed 1.0 for long documents due to normalization bug
description: The recency-weighted reranker computes a combined score as semantic_score + recency_bonus. The recency_bonus calculation divides the staleness weight by a constant that was set to 0.5 instead of 1.0 during an early prototype, doubling all recency bonuses and causing combined scores to exceed the expected [0, 1] range. This inflates ranking of recent documents beyond the intended weighting.

title: Answer agent prompt template missing context window guard
description: When the retriever returns a large number of long document chunks, the combined prompt to the answering agent exceeds Claude Sonnet's context window limit and the API call fails with a context length error. A token-counting pre-check that trims the context to a safe limit (e.g. 80,000 tokens) before prompt assembly prevents the error.

title: Ingestion script fails silently on malformed JSON without error logging
description: When the input JSON file contains a record with a missing required field such as 'id' or 'created_date', the ingestion script catches the KeyError internally but logs nothing and continues, resulting in partial ingestion with no visibility into which records were skipped. Replacing the bare except block with explicit field validation and structured error logging resolves the observability gap.

title: Milvus gRPC connection drops silently after 10 minutes of idle time
description: The Milvus Python SDK's gRPC channel uses a default keepalive idle timeout that is shorter than the Docker network's connection idle timeout. After 10 minutes of inactivity, the channel is torn down by the network layer, but the SDK does not reconnect automatically on the next call — it raises a connection error instead. Setting the keepalive_time_ms and keepalive_timeout_ms channel options resolves the silent disconnect.

title: Stale embedding cache causes wrong retrieval after document re-ingestion
description: An in-memory LRU cache stores embeddings keyed by document ID. When a document is updated and re-ingested with the same ID, the cache still returns the old embedding for any query that hits the cache before the TTL expires. The cache key must include a hash of the document content, or the cache entry must be invalidated on ingestion of a matching document ID.

title: Planner agent misclassifies time-sensitive queries as static retrieval type
description: Queries that include recency signals such as 'latest', 'current', or 'as of today' are not being recognized by the Planner as requiring recency-weighted retrieval. The Planner's classification prompt does not enumerate these keywords as triggers for the temporal retrieval path. As a result, stale documents may be returned without applying the recency reranking pass.

title: Postgres timestamp columns are not timezone-aware causing date sort errors
description: The 'created_date' and 'updated_date' columns in the document_metadata table were created as TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE. When comparing documents ingested from different sources with mixed UTC offsets, the sort order is incorrect. Migrating the columns to TIMESTAMPTZ and converting all stored values to UTC eliminates the ordering inconsistency.

title: bge-m3 model fails to load after Ollama minor version upgrade
description: After upgrading Ollama from v0.3.1 to v0.3.6, the bge-m3 model fails to load with 'model requires GGUF format v3' error. The newer Ollama version changed the expected GGUF format and the cached model file is incompatible. Running 'ollama pull bge-m3' after the upgrade fetches the compatible format and resolves the issue.

title: Docker volume mount causes permission error on Milvus data directory
description: On first run, the Docker Compose Milvus container fails to start with 'permission denied' when writing to the host-mounted ./volumes/milvus directory. The Milvus process runs as UID 1000 inside the container, but the mounted directory is owned by root on the host. Running 'sudo chown -R 1000:1000 ./volumes' before first startup resolves the issue.

title: Retriever returns empty results when query string contains regex metacharacters
description: Queries containing characters such as parentheses, brackets, or plus signs are passed directly into a Milvus expression filter string without escaping, causing a Milvus expression parse error that silently returns an empty result set. Sanitizing query strings before interpolating them into Milvus filter expressions prevents the parse failure.

title: Recency reranking weight too aggressive — penalizes highly relevant older documents
description: The recency reranking formula applies a logarithmic decay on document age but the decay constant was calibrated on a synthetic dataset where all documents were within 6 months old. On the full dataset spanning 24 months, documents older than 12 months receive near-zero combined scores even when their semantic similarity is very high. The decay constant needs to be tuned against the actual date distribution of the corpus.

title: OpenRouter response JSON parsing fails on non-standard model output format
description: When Claude Sonnet includes a code block in its response, the answer agent's JSON parser fails because it expects the model to return only a JSON object, not Markdown-wrapped JSON. Adding a pre-processing step that strips Markdown fences before parsing resolves the issue. The prompt template should also be updated to explicitly instruct the model to omit Markdown formatting.

title: Ingestion pipeline does not handle Confluence pages with only embedded image content
description: Confluence pages that consist entirely of embedded images with no text body produce empty string content after HTML parsing. The ingestion script does not filter these out and attempts to embed an empty string, which causes the Ollama API to return a zero-vector. Zero-vectors pollute the Milvus index and may appear as false positives for any query vector. Pages with content length less than 20 characters should be skipped with a logged warning.

title: Milvus timeout root cause confirmed and permanently resolved via HNSW index
description: Root cause analysis of JIRA-1001 is now confirmed: the IVF_FLAT index was configured with nlist=32, far too low for a collection of 50,000+ vectors, causing exhaustive search on each query. The correct fix is to rebuild the index using HNSW with M=16 and ef_construction=200. The previous 60-second timeout workaround must be reverted to 10 seconds. Post-fix p95 search latency is 1.1 seconds, down from 27 seconds. The batch_size=4 restriction from JIRA-1001 is no longer needed.

title: Ollama OOM properly resolved: correct environment flags eliminate batch size restriction
description: The JIRA-1002 workaround of reducing batch size to 4 was overly conservative. The actual root cause was Ollama loading multiple model instances in parallel when OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL was not set, tripling memory consumption. Setting OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1 and OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1 in the Docker environment caps peak memory at 18GB on a 32GB host. Batch size of 8 is stable and correct; batch size of 4 is no longer recommended and should be reverted in all ingestion configurations.

title: Add Jira ticket type filter to retrieval API request parameters
description: The retrieval API currently returns all document types (bug, feature_request, task) regardless of user intent. Adding an optional 'jira_type' filter parameter would let callers restrict results to a single ticket type, reducing noise in answers about specific categories of work. The filter should be passed as a Milvus scalar expression on the 'type' field.

title: Implement embedding cache with configurable TTL for repeated queries
description: Computing embeddings for repeated or near-duplicate queries adds unnecessary latency and CPU load. An LRU cache keyed on the normalized query string with a configurable TTL (default 5 minutes) would eliminate redundant Ollama calls for the same query. Cache size and TTL should be configurable via environment variables.

title: Include source document metadata and confidence score in answer API response
description: The current answer API returns only the generated text with no indication of which documents were retrieved or how confident the system is. Adding a 'sources' array to the response — including document ID, title, updated_date, and reranker score — would let consumers display provenance information and build trust in the answer quality.

title: Support multi-space Confluence search scoped by space key
description: Users asking questions scoped to a specific Confluence space (e.g. 'What does the Platform team's runbook say about...') cannot currently restrict retrieval to that space. Adding an optional 'confluence_space' filter that maps to the 'space' scalar field in Milvus would allow scoped retrieval without changing the embedding pipeline.

title: Add async batch embedding endpoint to reduce ingestion wall-clock time
description: The current ingestion pipeline embeds one document at a time over a synchronous HTTP call to Ollama. Adding an async batch endpoint that submits all documents concurrently (with a configurable concurrency limit) and collects results would reduce total ingestion time from O(n) sequential to O(n/concurrency). This is especially important for the planned Phase 2 scale-up to 10,000+ documents.

title: Implement streaming response from answering agent via SSE
description: The answering agent currently waits for the full OpenRouter response before returning to the caller, adding perceived latency proportional to answer length. Implementing Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming would allow the frontend or API consumer to receive tokens as they are generated, improving perceived responsiveness for long answers.

title: Display retrieved document snippet alongside generated answer
description: Returning the generated answer without any excerpt from the source documents makes it difficult to verify correctness. Adding a 'context_snippets' field to the API response — containing the top 3 retrieved chunks with their document IDs and titles — would allow users to quickly cross-reference the answer against the source material.

title: Support natural language date range filters in user queries
description: Users should be able to query with phrases like 'Jira bugs created in the last 30 days' or 'Confluence pages updated since January'. The Planner agent should extract date filter intent and translate it into a Milvus scalar expression on the created_date or updated_date fields, enabling time-bounded retrieval without requiring the user to know the field names.

title: Add Jira project key scoping to retrieval pipeline requests
description: Enterprises running multiple Jira projects in the same corpus need to scope queries to a single project key (e.g. INFRA, KNOW, PLAT). Adding a 'jira_project' filter parameter that maps to a 'project' scalar field in Milvus would isolate retrieval to the relevant project without embedding changes.

title: Add thumbs-up/down user feedback collection on generated answers
description: Collecting explicit user feedback on answer quality would provide a labeled dataset for future reranker fine-tuning. A simple POST /feedback endpoint accepting query_id, answer_id, and rating (-1 or +1) with an optional free-text comment would be sufficient for the initial version. Feedback should be persisted in Postgres with the original query and retrieved document IDs for later analysis.

title: Add exponential backoff with jitter for all OpenRouter API calls
description: The current OpenRouter client retries failed calls immediately with a fixed delay, which can cause thundering herd behavior under sustained rate limiting. Implementing exponential backoff with jitter (base=1s, max=32s) and a maximum of 5 retries would improve resilience under load without overwhelming the API when the rate limit is already saturated.

title: Support incremental re-ingestion of updated documents without full collection rebuild
description: Re-ingesting an updated corpus currently requires dropping the entire Milvus collection and re-embedding all documents, which takes 30+ minutes for large datasets. Incremental re-ingestion — using document ID as a deduplication key to upsert only changed or new records — would reduce re-ingestion time proportionally to the change rate rather than corpus size.

title: Expose document freshness score as a visible field in retrieval response metadata
description: The recency bonus computed during reranking is currently internal and not surfaced in the API response. Exposing it as a 'freshness_score' field alongside the semantic score and combined score in the response metadata would make the ranking logic interpretable and aid debugging when retrieval results seem unexpectedly ordered.

title: Implement per-request distributed trace logging for end-to-end debugging
description: Currently, debugging a failed or low-quality answer requires correlating log lines across four separate agent modules using only a timestamp. Adding a trace_id (UUID) that is generated at query entry and propagated through every agent call, Milvus search, and LLM call would allow end-to-end tracing of a single request. Structured JSON logs keyed by trace_id would make correlation trivial.

title: Create Grafana dashboard for real-time retrieval pipeline latency metrics
description: There is currently no operational visibility into per-stage latency (embedding, vector search, reranking, LLM call) or error rates in the pipeline. A Grafana dashboard reading from a Prometheus metrics endpoint would allow operators to identify bottlenecks and detect regressions after deployments. The dashboard should show p50, p95, p99 latency histograms for each pipeline stage.

title: Support Markdown-formatted answer output in API response
description: The answering agent currently returns plain text answers. Instructing the LLM to format answers using Markdown (bold headings, bullet lists, code blocks) and preserving that formatting in the API response would improve readability in frontends that render Markdown natively.

title: Expose /health endpoint for all pipeline microservices
description: All pipeline components (embedding server, retrieval API, answer agent) currently lack a dedicated /health endpoint. Adding a /health GET route that checks component-specific dependencies (Milvus connectivity, Ollama status, Postgres connection) would enable Docker Compose health checks and external monitoring integrations.

title: Build A/B testing harness for comparing retrieval strategy variants
description: To systematically evaluate improvements to the retrieval strategy (e.g. different reranking weights, different chunk sizes, different index types), an A/B testing harness that routes a percentage of queries to an alternate pipeline configuration and compares results against a ground-truth answer set would accelerate experimentation.

title: Add content-hash-based document deduplication during ingestion
description: When the ingestion script is run multiple times on the same dataset (e.g. after adding new documents to the file), previously ingested documents are re-embedded and re-inserted, causing duplicates in Milvus. Computing an SHA-256 hash of the document content and checking it against a Postgres deduplication table before ingestion would prevent redundant processing and Milvus bloat.

title: Add query intent classifier to route temporal vs. factual retrieval modes
description: Not all queries benefit equally from recency-weighted reranking. Factual questions about stable concepts ('What is Milvus?') should use pure semantic retrieval, while operational questions ('What is the current rollback procedure?') should use recency-weighted retrieval. A lightweight intent classifier in the Planner — even a simple keyword heuristic — would route queries to the appropriate retrieval mode and avoid over-penalizing older but semantically correct documents.

title: Set up Docker Compose environment for Milvus, Etcd, and MinIO
description: Configure a Docker Compose file that brings up Milvus standalone mode with its required dependencies (Etcd for metadata and MinIO for object storage). Include health checks, named volumes for persistence, and a dedicated Docker network. Validate that the Milvus gRPC port 19530 and HTTP port 9091 are accessible from the host.

title: Configure bge-m3 model in Ollama and validate embedding output dimensions
description: Pull the bge-m3 model into the local Ollama instance and write a validation script that embeds a set of test strings and confirms output vector dimensionality (1024), dtype (float32), and L2 norm close to 1.0 for cosine similarity correctness. Document the Ollama API call format used by the embedding pipeline.

title: Design and document Milvus collection schema for Jira and Confluence data
description: Define the Milvus collection schema that stores both Jira ticket embeddings and Confluence page embeddings in a single collection, using a 'source_type' scalar field for filtering. Schema must include: vector field (dim=1024), id, title, source_type, priority (nullable), space (nullable), created_date (int64 Unix timestamp), updated_date (int64 Unix timestamp), and trap_group (nullable VARCHAR).

title: Author hand-crafted ground-truth dataset with deliberate trap pairs
description: Create the Phase 1 evaluation dataset: 60 Jira tickets, 60 Confluence pages, and a trap_manifest.json documenting 10 trap groups. Trap pairs must have semantically similar content on the same topic but different updated_dates, so that a date-naive retriever returns the wrong (stale) document while a recency-weighted retriever returns the correct (newer) document.

title: Implement baseline semantic-only retrieval pipeline end to end
description: Build the first complete pipeline version that uses only cosine similarity from Milvus (no recency reranking) to retrieve the top-k documents for a query. This baseline must be fully functional and demonstrably reproduce the staleness bug — specifically, for trap-group queries in the manifest, the outdated document must rank above the correct newer document consistently.

title: Write before/after demo script using trap_manifest.json queries
description: Create a runnable demo script that takes each query from the trap_manifest.json, runs it through both the broken baseline retriever and the fixed recency-weighted retriever, and prints a side-by-side comparison showing which document was ranked first by each retriever. The output should clearly show that the fixed retriever selects the correct_document_id for all trap groups.

title: Set up Postgres schema for document metadata and ingestion state tracking
description: Create the Postgres tables required by the ingestion pipeline: document_metadata (id, source_type, title, created_date, updated_date, content_hash, ingested_at) and ingestion_runs (run_id, started_at, completed_at, documents_processed, errors_count). Write the Alembic migration file to deploy the schema.

title: Write README as a technical case study for portfolio publication
description: Draft the project README as a structured technical case study covering: (1) Problem statement — the staleness bug in production, (2) Diagnosis — why semantic-only retrieval fails, (3) Architecture — the agentic pipeline components, (4) Fix — recency-weighted hybrid reranking with the actual scoring formula, (5) Results — before/after metrics on trap group queries. Target audience is senior engineers and technical interviewers.

title: Implement recency-weighted hybrid reranking scoring function
description: Build the reranker module that combines the Milvus cosine similarity score with a recency bonus based on updated_date. The combined score formula is: combined = alpha * semantic_score + (1 - alpha) * recency_score, where recency_score = exp(-lambda * days_since_update) and alpha/lambda are tunable parameters. Return results sorted by combined score descending.

title: Run load test to validate pipeline handles 100 concurrent queries
description: Use Locust to simulate 100 concurrent users submitting queries to the KnowOps API endpoint. Measure p50, p95, and p99 end-to-end latency, error rate, and resource utilization (CPU, RAM, GPU). Establish a performance baseline before Phase 2 scale-up. Target: p95 latency < 10 seconds, error rate < 1% under 100 concurrent users.
